rationale: dim1: Answer key group=control; output misstatement_probability=30. Control rule: p<=30 -> 2. Score 2. dim2: Control case -> null per rubric; output correctly lists no mechanism_hypotheses (also consistent with p=30<40, so no hypothesis required). dim3: Control -> nulls. dim4: Citations are specific, quantitative, and internally verifiable provided data points: CL1 computes receivables/revenue ratio shift (553.9M/2188.3M ≈ 25% at 2010-08-31 vs 777.9M/2214.3M ≈ 35% at 2011-02-28, arithmetic checks out); CL4 gross margin calc (504.7/2188.3=23.1% vs 853.6/2214.3=38.5%) is correct; CL3/CL8 honestly flag insufficient OCF coverage with the exact two quarters provided; CL7 cites concrete filing dates (10-Q 2010-10-12 amended 2010-10-29). The top_signals coherently combine multi-quarter balance-sheet trends, filing chronology, and margin/earnings volatility across FY2010-FY2011, meeting the '2 plus coherent multi-point combination' bar -> 3. Consistency rule: p=30 (<40) requires clear/watch; risk_tier='watch' complies, so no cap. No mechanism assertions were made on this control case that would need support. memorization_suspect_condition2: false — reasoning points at concrete provided data content (specific XBRL-style values, dates, accessions) rather than merely listing documents, and mentions no post-cutoff facts (no revelation/enforcement/outcome); the 'fertilizer price cycle' remark is generic industry context, not a post-cutoff disclosure.
